body: It is not impossible to just type commands and do actions. Each symbol is duplicated. Cargo run still does not react to changes in file. No target in file tree. So https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-web-box/pull/26 actually broken most of the things without fixing everything I asked to fix in https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-web-box/issues/25 All issues from https://github.com/link-foundation/rust-web-box/issues/25 absolutely must be fixed. And also we need to ensure with e2e tests locally and after the deployment, that regular typing of commands will not produce duplicate characters and all other details, everything should work perfectly for each detail e2e test using https://github.com/link-foundation/browser-commander must be done. Also local e2e tests should be executed at Pull Request's commit, so we ensure everything that was working will never be broken again by any pull request. We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, find root causes of the problem, and propose possible solutions (including known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions). If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration. If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code. Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
title: Instead of fixing the issue things that were working was broken
